description: Adversarial fixtures for design-audit GEPA. Mix of controlled local pages with planted defects + curated reference URLs. Local fixtures are loaded via file:// — no server needed.

fixtures.name: Workspace home with 6 equal-weight CTAs
fixtures.notes: Welcome screen has six equal-weight outline buttons. Audit must flag absent action hierarchy.

goldenFindings.hint: Six identical outlined buttons compete for attention; one should dominate.
The screen could be any startup; nothing is specific to the product.

fixtures.name: Dashboard with abstract metric cards and no domain objects
fixtures.notes: Cards labelled Total/Active/Pending/Errors with no units or domain context.

goldenFindings.hint: Total/Active/Pending/Errors are abstract; the audit should call out missing domain meaning.
User has nothing to do here — no next-action affordance.

fixtures.name: Payment confirmation hides fees and provider identity
fixtures.notes: $120 total with no fee/tax line, no merchant identity beyond a name, no payment method shown.

goldenFindings.hint: Order summary lacks fees/tax line items before commitment.
Acme Pay is unverified — no logo, no domain proof, no security indicator.
No card or payment method visible; user cannot tell how payment will be charged.

fixtures.name: Empty inbox with cute decorative empty state
fixtures.notes: Empty state shows a sparkle and a generic 'Nothing here' message instead of previewing the product.

goldenFindings.hint: Empty state uses a sparkle and platitude instead of a preview row, sample message, or setup checklist.
The 'Got it' button does not move the user toward any product outcome.

fixtures.name: Reports page that explains itself instead of showing reports
fixtures.notes: Body copy explains what the Reports page is supposed to be, with no real reports rendered.

goldenFindings.hint: Copy is meta — describes the page rather than helping the user act.
The product surface is empty scaffolding instead of real reports or starter state.

fixtures.name: Production deploy controls without risk surfacing
fixtures.notes: Deploy and Rollback buttons sit side by side, no environment label, no diff, no warning before commit.

goldenFindings.hint: Deploy is one click on production with no environment indicator, no diff, no confirmation.
Deploy and Rollback buttons have similar visual weight; the destructive option should be distinguished.

fixtures.name: Linear marketing — high-bar reference
fixtures.notes: Reference high-quality page. Recall here is OPTIONAL — used as a stability and ceiling check.

fixtures.name: Stripe marketing — high-bar reference
fixtures.notes: Reference high-quality page; bound the ceiling so GEPA doesn't optimise toward false-positive verbosity.
